content.text: Resuming Execution

Loading a checkpoint is the easy part. The hard part is deciding what to do with it. External state may have changed while the agent was paused — records may have been modified, tokens may have expired, files may have moved. A resume strategy that ignores these changes can produce incorrect results or corrupt data. This document covers how to validate and rehydrate agent state correctly before continuing a paused run.

attrs.alt: Resuming execution decision tree: direct resume, refresh and resume, discard and restart, or abort

content.text: The Four Resume Strategies
Strategy
When to use
Cost
Direct resume
External state unchanged; pause was short
Lowest — restore snapshot and continue
Refresh and resume
Some external data is stale but re-fetchable
Medium — re-query affected resources before continuing
Discard and restart
Snapshot too stale; context no longer valid
High — full re-run but correct
Abort and notify
Task cannot continue in any safe way
Terminal — require human decision on next steps
Defaulting to 'direct resume' without checking external state is the most common mistake. Always validate the staleness of any resource the agent was working with before continuing.
Staleness Checks
A staleness check compares the current state of an external resource against the state recorded in the snapshot. The check must run before the agent emits any new tool calls:
Resume Coordinator
The coordinator runs all staleness checks and returns the appropriate strategy. It is called before re-entering the agent loop:
The Resume Entry Point

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

async def resume_agent(
    snapshot_id: str,
    store: "CheckpointStore",
    tools: list[dict],
    system: str,
    pause_token: asyncio.Event,
) -> str:
    snapshot = await store.load(snapshot_id)
    if snapshot is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Snapshot {snapshot_id} not found")

    decision = await decide_resume_strategy(snapshot)

    if decision.strategy == ResumeStrategy.ABORT:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Resume aborted: {decision.reason}")

    if decision.strategy == ResumeStrategy.RESTART:
        # Discard snapshot; caller must re-run from the beginning
        await store.delete(snapshot_id)
        raise RuntimeError(f"Snapshot discarded; restart required: {decision.reason}")

    messages = snapshot.messages
    work_queue = snapshot.work_queue
    session_approved = set(snapshot.session_approved)

    if decision.strategy == ResumeStrategy.REFRESH:
        # Inject a system-level note about what changed so the model has context
        refresh_note = (
            f"[RESUME CONTEXT] The following resources changed while this agent was paused: "
            f"{decision.reason}. Updated data has been loaded."
        )
        messages = messages + [{"role": "user", "content": refresh_note}]

    # Re-enter the agent loop from the saved iteration
    for iteration in range(snapshot.iteration, 100):
        if pause_token.is_set():
            new_snap = AgentSnapshot(
                agent_id=snapshot.agent_id,
                iteration=iteration,
                messages=messages,
                work_queue=work_queue,
                session_approved=list(session_approved),
                metadata=snapshot.metadata,
            )
            new_id = await store.save(new_snap)
            raise AgentPaused(new_id)

        resp = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-opus-4-8",
            max_tokens=4096,
            system=system,
            messages=messages,
            tools=tools,
        )
        if resp.stop_reason == "end_turn":
            await store.delete(snapshot_id)
            return " ".join(b.text for b in resp.content if hasattr(b, "text"))

        tool_results = []
        for block in resp.content:
            if block.type != "tool_use":
                continue
            result = await _execute_tool(block.name, block.input)
            tool_results.append({"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": block.id, "content": result})

        messages = messages + [
            {"role": "assistant", "content": resp.content},
            {"role": "user", "content": tool_results},
        ]

    return "max_iterations reached"
Tracking Watched Resources in Snapshots

For staleness checks to work, the agent must record what external resources it depends on at checkpoint time. The cleanest approach is to intercept tool calls that read external state and log the resource ID plus its version:
Idempotency on Resume
If an agent paused after completing some tool calls but before recording them in the conversation, re-running those calls on resume would duplicate side effects. Avoid this by:
Only pausing after recording tool results
— always add the tool result to the message list before saving the checkpoint. This ensures the model cannot re-request an already-completed call.
Using idempotency keys
— pass a unique key derived from the snapshot ID and iteration number when calling external APIs. The API deduplicates on the key.
Checking done-markers
— for work-queue-based agents, mark each item DONE in persistent storage before removing it from the in-memory queue. On resume, skip DONE items.
Testing Resume Paths

import pytest
import asyncio

async def test_direct_resume():
    store = FileCheckpointStore(".test_checkpoints")
    snap = AgentSnapshot(
        agent_id="test-agent",
        iteration=5,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Continue from here"}],
        metadata={"watched_resources": [], "restartable": True},
    )
    sid = await store.save(snap)

    decision = await decide_resume_strategy(snap)
    assert decision.strategy == ResumeStrategy.DIRECT

    await store.delete(sid)

async def test_abort_on_deleted_resource(monkeypatch):
    async def mock_check(*args, **kwargs):
        return StalenessResult(stale=True, reason="Record deleted", refreshed_value=None)

    monkeypatch.setattr("mymodule.check_record_staleness", mock_check)

    snap = AgentSnapshot(
        agent_id="test-agent",
        iteration=3,
        messages=[],
        metadata={
            "watched_resources": [{"id": "rec-1", "etag": "abc"}],
            "restartable": False,
        },
    )
    decision = await decide_resume_strategy(snap)
    assert decision.strategy == ResumeStrategy.ABORT
Context Window Management on Resume
After many iterations, the restored message history may approach the model's context window limit. Before re-entering the loop, check message size and summarise if needed:

import tiktoken  # or use a character estimate

def estimate_tokens(messages: list[dict]) -> int:
    # Rough estimate: 1 token ≈ 4 characters
    total_chars = sum(len(str(m)) for m in messages)
    return total_chars // 4

MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS = 150_000   # leave headroom for response

async def trim_messages_for_resume(
    messages: list[dict],
    system: str,
    client: "anthropic.Anthropic",
) -> list[dict]:
    estimated = estimate_tokens(messages)
    if estimated <= MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS:
        return messages

    # Summarise everything except the last 20 turns
    keep_recent = messages[-40:]   # last 20 pairs (user+assistant)
    old_messages = messages[:-40]

    summary_prompt = (
        "Summarise the following conversation history in 3-5 bullet points. "
        "Focus on: what was accomplished, what decisions were made, and what remains to do.\n\n"
        + "\n".join(f"{m['role']}: {str(m.get('content', ''))[:500]}" for m in old_messages)
    )
    summary_resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=512,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": summary_prompt}],
    )
    summary_text = summary_resp.content[0].text

    trimmed = [
        {"role": "user", "content": f"[CONVERSATION SUMMARY] {summary_text}"},
        {"role": "assistant", "content": "Understood. I'll continue from where we left off."},
    ] + keep_recent

    return trimmed
Always use a cheaper/faster model (e.g. Haiku) for summary generation on resume — this is a mechanical task, not a reasoning task, and the cost saving is significant for long-running agents.
Human Correction on Resume
When a human resumes an agent after a pause, they may want to correct the agent's direction before it continues. Support this by accepting an optional correction message at the resume endpoint:

from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional

app = FastAPI()

class ResumeRequest(BaseModel):
    correction: Optional[str] = None   # human-provided direction change

@app.post("/agents/{agent_id}/resume/{snapshot_id}")
async def http_resume(agent_id: str, snapshot_id: str, body: ResumeRequest):
    snapshot = await store.load(snapshot_id)
    if not snapshot or snapshot.agent_id != agent_id:
        raise HTTPException(404)

    messages = snapshot.messages
    if body.correction:
        messages = messages + [{
            "role": "user",
            "content": f"[HUMAN CORRECTION on resume] {body.correction}",
        }]

    # Rebuild snapshot with corrected messages before resuming
    corrected = AgentSnapshot(
        agent_id=agent_id,
        snapshot_id=snapshot_id,
        created_at=snapshot.created_at,
        iteration=snapshot.iteration,
        messages=messages,
        work_queue=snapshot.work_queue,
        session_approved=snapshot.session_approved,
        metadata=snapshot.metadata,
    )

    asyncio.create_task(
        resume_agent(
            snapshot_id=snapshot_id,
            store=store,
            tools=MY_TOOLS,
            system=_get_system_prompt(agent_id),
            pause_token=asyncio.Event(),
        )
    )
    return {"status": "resuming", "correction_applied": bool(body.correction)}
Common Pitfalls on Resume
Pitfall
Symptom
Fix
Skipping staleness checks
Agent modifies already-changed records
Always run decide_resume_strategy() before entering the loop
Injecting refresh note after old messages
Model ignores note buried in long history
Prepend refresh note as first user message, not last
Context window overflow on restore
API error: prompt too long
Run trim_messages_for_resume() before first model call
Holding old session_approved after restart
Bypassed approvals carry over inappropriately
Clear session_approved when strategy is RESTART or ABORT
Not deleting snapshot after success
Stale checkpoints accumulate
Delete checkpoint in the finally block after successful completion
Summary

Resuming an agent requires more than restoring a snapshot and calling the model again. Validate external state before resuming: if resources are current, resume directly; if stale but refreshable, re-fetch and inject context; if too stale, restart or abort. Track watched resources in the snapshot metadata so staleness checks have something to compare. Always ensure tool results are recorded in the message list before taking the checkpoint — this prevents duplicate side effects on resume.
